project.name: Cliffwatch Mansion Architectural Program
project.description: A two-floor neo-gothic mansion program for deterministic orthogonal blockout planning.

intent.summary: Plan a coherent, editable two-floor neo-gothic mansion blockout around a legible central spine and one aligned stair core.
mustHave: A grand foyer with an exterior entrance and direct access to the ballroom
A formal dining room directly connected to the service kitchen
A private upper floor connected by one aligned stair
Every room explicitly reachable from the exterior entrance
mustNotHave: Pre-authored Roblox geometry or executable behavior
preferences: Keep public rooms toward the entrance
Keep private rooms away from the entrance
Pair the library with the drawing room when capacity permits
Give every room useful exterior windows

references.role: Observed neo-gothic frontage, vertical rhythm, and coastal massing
references.notes: The fixture records only an abstract authored observation; no private image or asset is included.

references.role: Authored room program, circulation requirements, and adjacency brief
references.notes: Defines the requested room uses and relationships without prescribing a floor plan.

references.role: Style DNA for a restrained neo-gothic architectural blockout
references.notes: Guides semantic material and shape language rather than exact hidden geometry.

A large public room is inferred from the frontage rhythm and required by the authored program.
The exact room geometry is invented to realize the authored entertaining program.
The public room is inferred from the observed facade bays; its interior placement remains a planning decision.
The secondary bedroom is invented to complete the residential program.
A principal public level is inferred from the observed entrance and the program.
The upper window course is directly evident, while its room division is not.
The foyer follows the observed primary entrance, but its clear-space geometry is inferred.
The shared private sitting room is invented to make the upper program coherent.
The service room is programmatically required but not visible in the elevation evidence.
The hidden room arrangement is invented; only its atmosphere follows the style brief.
The overall two-story frontage is observed; the exact dimensions and hidden plan remain authored planning inputs.
A bounded house parcel is inferred around the observed mansion frontage.
The bath is invented to complete the requested primary suite.
A principal upper room is inferred from the elevation hierarchy; its layout is planned.
The coastal cliff setting is directly represented in the authored elevation observation.
A single aligned stair core is invented to satisfy explicit vertical circulation.
The compact study is invented as the final ground-floor program space.
The room is invented as a quiet upper-floor work and dressing space.
The secondary bedroom is invented to complete the residential program.
The world boundary combines the observed estate context with the authored blockout scope.
